response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic), plus strong Linux and testing discipline. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and the candidate's C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a six-month stint in 2014-2015), with the last several years focused on TypeScript/Node web development. There is no direct routing, navigation, or map-tiling pipeline experience, so the fit hinges on how transferable the DLang systems background proves to be.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience through founding and maintaining an open-source GIS platform
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work with async I/O, caching, and fibers in DLang
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, and authored testing tooling and workshops
concerns: Modern C++/Rust experience is minimal and roughly a decade old
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or tile-generation pipeline background
Recent roles (2020-present) are web/full-stack TypeScript, distant from systems-level pipeline work
